groups.context_description: Term names the inner refreshment of the satisfied soul — the prosperity and fat-richness of the inner person whose desire is fulfilled
Term names the anointing that signals divine presence and favour — the saturation of the head with oil as the emblem of divine blessing upon the inner person
Term names the anointing of inner perception — the invitation to receive what opens the eyes of the heart to see one's true spiritual condition
Term names the anointed guardian cherub as the one whose privileged inner access to God was corrupted by pride — anointing as the mark of exceptional inner standing before God
Term names the anointing that confers the Spirit — the act of anointing as the occasion of the Spirit rushing upon the person, transforming the inner life
Term names the sacred inviolability of the anointed — the inner prohibition against harming the one whom God has set apart through anointing
Term names anointing as divine appointment and blessing — the act that designates the person for a calling and marks divine favour upon them
Term names the anointing oil as the sacred medium of consecration — the oil prepared and applied in the act of setting apart persons and objects for divine service
Term names the anointing oil as the sacred medium of consecration — the oil prepared and applied in the act of setting apart persons and objects for divine service
Term names the anointed priest whose inner condition carries covenantal weight — the priest whose sin or purity affects the whole community
Term names the anointed king as the person set apart for divine rule — the one whom God has chosen and whose protection is sacred
Term names the anointed one whose coming is the fulfilment of divine promise — Messiah as the eschatological figure whose appearance marks the consummation of God's purpose
Term names the deliberate abstention from anointing as a practice of mourning — not anointing as the outward expression of inner grief before God
Term names the anointing of restoration — God anointing the person as the act of claiming and beautifying what had been degraded
Term names the anointing of the eyes as the restoration of inner perception — the outer act whose purpose is the return of the capacity to see
Oil as metaphor for quality and character of the inner person
Oil of gladness — inner joy and divine bestowing of flourishing
Oil as outward sign of divine appointment and inner consecration
Oil as marker of disordered inner orientation — self-indulgence and spiritual unfaithfulness
Oil as image of inner penetration — reaching the deepest interior of the person
Term names anointing in anticipation of death — the act of love that prepares the body of Christ for burial, performed in advance through devotion
Term names the antichrist as the paradigm of inner spiritual deception — the spirit of denial as the inner orientation that refuses to acknowledge Christ
Term names the making fat of the inner heart as the judgment that removes perception — God causing the heart to become dull and unresponsive to his voice
Term names the spiritual fatness of prosperity that turns the heart from God — abundance producing inner dullness and self-sufficiency in place of dependence
Term names the blotting out of sin as the erasure of the inner record — anointing-reversal as the act of forgiveness that wipes away what stands against the person
Term names the eschatological wiping away of tears and sorrow — the final reversal of all inner suffering in the renewed creation
Term names the anointing oil as the vehicle of devotion — the costly perfume offered as the expression of inner love and honour toward Christ
Term names the satisfaction of the soul — the inner fullness of delight when the person is nourished by what God provides
Term names the flourishing of the inner person in old age — the fat and fresh quality of the soul that continues to bear fruit through sustained trust in God
Term names the anointing of the Spirit as inner knowledge and protection — the anointing that remains in the person and teaches them from within, guarding against deception
Term names the anointing of the Spirit as inner knowledge and protection — the anointing that remains in the person and teaches them from within, guarding against deception
Term names the Christ as the anointed one whose identity is the object of inner faith — the person of Jesus as the ground of life through belief
Term names union with Christ as the defining inner reality of the person — being in Christ as the location of the transformed inner life

Term names the death and resurrection of Christ as the inner transformative foundation — Christ died for sins; this is received truth on which the inner life rests and on which hope of resurrection stands
Term names the anointing of Jesus with the Holy Spirit as the equipping for inner authority and action — Spirit-reception as the source of inner capacity for ministry
Term names the anointing of the believer by God as the ground of inner certainty — divine anointing as the establishing act that makes the person firm in faith
Term names anointing as an expression of honour, love, and hospitality — the outer act of anointing as the vehicle of inner regard for the person
Term names anointing as the designated means of healing prayer — the outer act accompanying faith and intercession in the community of faith
Term names the crown as the symbol of the consecrated or covenantal state — the outward sign of inner dedication and holy standing before God
Term names the consecrated hair as the symbol of dedication to God — its cutting away marking the inner rupture of consecration through divine rejection
Term names the act of self-separation or dedication — the inner movement of consecrating oneself to God or, inversely, separating oneself from him
Term names the voluntary inner commitment of total dedication to God — the Nazirite vow as the outward form of an inner act of consecration of the whole person
Term names the dedication of persons or places to God as an expression of covenantal belonging — the act of setting something apart for God that reflects the inner orientation of covenant loyalty
Term names the manifestation of divine holiness — the revealing of God's inner character of holiness before the nations and Israel, which calls for recognition and inner response
Term names the sanctuary as the space of divine presence — the place that calls forth and shapes the inner orientation of reverence, awe, and consecrated approach toward God
Term names the Holy Place as the threshold of divine encounter — the approach to which demands inner readiness, solemnity, and the bearing of responsibility before God
Term names the divine spirit's indwelling as the source of inner wisdom, discernment, and holy capacity in the person
Term designates the saints of the Most High — those whose inner identity and destiny is constituted by belonging to the holy God
Term designates those whose inner life is set apart to God — the holy person or saint whose character, orientation, and standing before God constitutes their defining quality
Term names the cult prostitute practice as the marker of inner spiritual adultery — the violation of covenantal fidelity that exposes moral hypocrisy in the community
Term names the Way of Holiness as the path accessible only to those whose inner condition has been purified — holiness as the defining inner criterion for participation in the divine way
Term names the state of consecration itself — the dedicated condition of the person who has set themselves apart to God, whose inner life is wholly oriented toward this commitment
Term names the divine act of consecrating persons — setting them apart through his own holiness to belong to him, constituting their inner identity as holy before God
Term names the inner act of consecrating oneself — the voluntary self-dedication and orientation of the whole person toward God in holy service
Term names the sanctification of time — keeping the Sabbath holy as an inner orientation of the person toward the rhythm of sacred rest and divine encounter
Term names holiness as an inner moral quality of the person — the character of being set apart inwardly from defilement, expressed in conduct and qualifying the person for service and worship

Term names holiness as the inner moral character and set-apart orientation by which the person is consecrated to God — the quality of the person's inner life that corresponds to God's own holiness and is required for approach to him
Term names the person's inner response — reverence, worship, and desire — evoked by encountering the holiness of God in his sanctuary or name
Term names the Spirit's holiness as the divine inner presence that grieves over human rebellion and is placed within the people by God — the inner holy presence as relational reality

Term names sanctification as the inner moral condition and transformative process by which a person is set apart for God — the inward character of holiness as the goal of Christian life and the fruit of the Spirit's work
Term names holiness as the inner moral quality of the person actively pursued and established in the heart — the condition of being set apart inwardly before God
Term names holiness as the inner moral character of the renewed person — the quality of the whole life lived before God, paired with righteousness as constitutive of the new self
Term names holiness as the inner moral character God calls the person to embody — the imperative to be holy as God is holy, grounding the person's inner life in the character of God

Term names the inner orientation of the person toward the Holy One — the response of the inner being (trust, awe, joy, rejection, unwillingness) directed toward God's holiness as a living relational reality
Term names holiness as the identity-constituting set-apart status of the person before God — the person's sacred standing that shapes who they are in relation to God and community
Term names holiness as the divine character quality that the person is disciplined to share — an inner transformation making the person a partaker of God's own holiness
Term names the idol as the object of misplaced inner orientation — the thing toward which the person's allegiance, rejoicing, or worship is wrongly directed
Term names the idol in relation to inner conviction or conscience — in theological understanding, moral formation, or the incompatibility of idol and God within the person
Term names food sacrificed to idols as the occasion of an inner conscience crisis — the act of eating or abstaining governed by the conscience, whose strength or weakness determines the moral outcome
Term names the idolater as a person whose inner orientation is toward created things rather than God — a defining inner condition with eschatological consequences
Term names the idol's temple as the site where a brother's weak conscience can be damaged — the location triggers an inner crisis of conscience in the weak
Term names a city full of idols — the external condition that provokes an inner response of the spirit, disclosing the person's inner sensitivity to God's honour
Term names idolatry as an inner orientation — the displacement of God by created things as the object of the heart's ultimate allegiance, whether in covetousness, passion, or formal worship
Term names prophecy as a spiritual gift given through the Spirit — the capacity to receive and speak divine disclosure, distinct from human will and assessed against love
Term names prophecy as the revelatory word preserved in Scripture and Revelation — the divinely authorised word to which faithful response is required
Term names the prophetic word as a communication that engages or targets the inner life — heard prophecy moves to courage and action; false prophecy targets and manipulates inner disposition
Term names the prophet as one specially oriented toward God — called to receive and declare his word, with an inner orientation that precedes and shapes their speech
Term names the prophet as the one who suffers for God's word — the inner-being posture of endurance under rejection and persecution as the mark of authentic prophecy
Term names the prophetic canon — the Law and the Prophets as the scriptural testimony that orients the inner life toward God's purposes
Term names prophesying as Spirit-enabled inner-being speech — the act of declaring divine truth through the Holy Spirit's movement on and in the person
Term names the communal exercise of prophecy — its purpose, order, and effect in building up the inner life of the congregation
Term names the fulfilment and testing of prophetic speech — both the historical fulfilment of prophetic words and the mockery that tests what prophecy truly is
Term names the false prophet as an inwardly deceptive figure — whose inner orientation is predatory, self-serving, or demonic, masked by an outward appearance of authenticity
Term names the eschatological False Prophet — the end-times figure of deception who drives worship of the beast, ultimately destroyed with him
Term names the prophetic written word as the inner-being lamp — the Scripture-canon of prophecy that illuminates the inner life until Christ fully dawns within
Term names the prophetess — whether the authentic inner calling or the false claim to prophetic authority used for seduction

Term identifies the act of prophesying as a Spirit-empowered inner event — the Spirit of God resting on or rushing upon a person activates an inward responsive capacity that issues in prophetic expression
Term identifies prophesying as expression of a true inner commission and orientation toward God — the genuine prophet speaks from inward faithfulness to divine word
Term identifies prophesying as expression of inner deceit and corrupt orientation — false prophets speak from their own hearts, conceiving and uttering from an inward disposition of falsehood
Term identifies the prophetic act as obedient expression of divine commission — the prophet's inner responsiveness to God's word issues in directed speech against persons, places, or nations
Title identifies a person whose identity and function is the mediation of divine inner-being communication — expressed in prophetic guidance, worship leadership, and prophetic manipulation
Term identifies prophesying as an inner-originating expression that sustains and motivates the inner disposition of hearers toward covenantal action
Term names sorcery as an inner-spiritual practice of rebellion — the orientation that seeks supernatural power outside of God, listed as a work of the flesh or a mark of spiritual apostasy
Term names the practice of sorcery — the act of engaging occult power as an expression of the inner spiritual orientation that rejects God's sole authority
Term names sorcery as a spiritual-relational practice — the use of occult means as a substitute for God, with specific inner-moral consequences
Term names the sorcerer as a character type — one whose inner-spiritual orientation is defined by occult practice, and who leads others away from God through false counsel
The testimony as the object of inner heart-engagement — the divine testimony to which the heart is inclined, which the soul keeps, which is the ground of whole-hearted covenant renewal
Testimony sealed within disciples — the divine testimony committed to an inner circle, with spiritual condition (having or lacking dawn) as the measure of reception
Signs as the direct conditions of inner belief — miraculous signs that function to produce or test faith, with belief or unbelief as the inner-being response explicitly tied to the sign
The word as inner-dwelling and conscience-addressing — the word of Christ taking up residence within, addressing the conscience, or expressing/revealing the inner being of the person

Witnessing as inner moral act and relational accountability — the bearing of witness (true or false) as an expression of inner integrity; God as inner-relational witness; prophetic witness calling to inner repentance

Prophetic testimony as directed at inner orientation — the divine and prophetic warning that calls to inner repentance, obedience, and spiritual receptivity; including testimony that reveals inner hardness or receptivity

Divine testimonies as the object of inner devotion — the testimonies kept with the whole heart, delighted in, clung to, and received as counselors; the inner states of joy, delight, and steadfast love that the testimonies evoke
Inner steadfastness in the face of external snare — not forgetting the law as an act of inner faithfulness despite constraint
Unbelief as an inner condition of the heart — described, rebuked, or personally wrestled with
Unbelief as a persistent inner state with decisive consequences — for individuals and communities
Wilful inner refusal — disobedience as an act of the will toward God, his Son, or his word
Unbelief as a persistent inner state with communal and relational consequences — hardness, poisoning of minds, mercy withheld or extended
dē intensifying an inner-being directive or volitional divine command
Term names the inner act of judging and distinguishing — morally discerning between persons or things, including wrongful discrimination
groups.notes: Description updated to match verse_context_group correction — VCB-022 80-char truncation restored

groups.context_description: Term names doubting as the inner division of the self — the heart split between faith and hesitation, driven and tossed like a wave
groups.notes: Description updated to match verse_context_group correction — VCB-022 80-char truncation restored

groups.context_description: Term names tearing as the expression of violent inner states — divine wrath, human predatory character, destructive anger — and the experience of being torn as what the inner person suffers under judgment or hostile power
groups.notes: Description updated to match verse_context_group correction — VCB-022 80-char truncation restored

groups.context_description: Term names the inner affirmation of truth — the "truly" that anchors a moral assertion, a confession, or a conviction about God's character
groups.notes: Description updated to match verse_context_group correction — VCB-022 80-char truncation restored

groups.context_description: Term names doubt as the inner experience of wavering — faith faltering between trust and hesitation — addressed directly by Jesus
groups.notes: Description updated to match verse_context_group correction — VCB-022 80-char truncation restored

groups.context_description: Term intensifies inner-being certainty, conviction, and commitment — the surely/certainly that names the firmness of moral judgment, apostolic conviction, and wholehearted resolve
groups.notes: Description updated to match verse_context_group correction — VCB-022 80-char truncation restored

groups.context_description: Term names discernment as an inner spiritual capacity — trained by practice to distinguish good from evil, or exercised as a gift to distinguish spirits
groups.notes: Description updated to match verse_context_group correction — VCB-022 80-char truncation restored

groups.context_description: Term names impartiality as a defining inner character quality of wisdom from above — the absence of inner wavering or prejudice in the person of wisdom
groups.notes: Description updated to match verse_context_group correction — VCB-022 80-char truncation restored

groups.context_description: Term names prey in its inner-being dimension — the predatory inner character of unjust leaders and oppressors, God's fierce resolve to fight for his people, and his covenantal provision for those who fear him
groups.notes: Description updated to match verse_context_group correction — VCB-022 80-char truncation restored

groups.context_description: Term names the torn animal as the boundary that tests inner integrity — the person who refuses defilement, bears losses rather than shifting blame, or whose predatory character fills its dens with what it has seized
groups.notes: Description updated to match verse_context_group correction — VCB-022 80-char truncation restored

groups.context_description: Term names the fresh-plucked olive leaf as the sign of renewed hope — the communicative act that produces inner recognition of grace and new beginning
groups.notes: Description updated to match verse_context_group correction — VCB-022 80-char truncation restored

groups.context_description: Term names the constancy of the inner-being orientation — always praying, always giving thanks, always rejoicing, always abounding — as the mark of a life wholly directed toward God
groups.notes: Description updated to match verse_context_group correction — VCB-022 80-char truncation restored

groups.context_description: Term names God as the Almighty — the All-Sovereign whose absolute power is the object of worship, the ground of covenant relationship, and the authority over all history and judgment
groups.notes: Description updated to match verse_context_group correction — VCB-022 80-char truncation restored

groups.context_description: Term names the universal scope of the gospel's reach — preached, commanded, and received everywhere — disclosing the inner-being mandate that has no geographic boundary
groups.notes: Description updated to match verse_context_group correction — VCB-022 80-char truncation restored

groups.context_description: Term names completeness in its inner-being dimension — the complete inability of the bound, and the complete sufficiency of Christ's saving power
groups.notes: Description updated to match verse_context_group correction — VCB-022 80-char truncation restored

groups.context_description: Term names the complete encirclement — being hemmed in on every side — as the inner-being experience of inescapable judgment
groups.notes: Description updated to match verse_context_group correction — VCB-022 80-char truncation restored

groups.context_description: Term names the universal drawing of people toward Jesus from every quarter — the inner orientation of seeking that comes from all sides
groups.notes: Description updated to match verse_context_group correction — VCB-022 80-char truncation restored

groups.context_description: Term names the blessing of God — pronouncing his name over creation, persons, bread — as the inner act of consecration and thankfulness
groups.notes: Description updated to match verse_context_group correction — VCB-022 80-char truncation restored

groups.context_description: Term names the blessing declared over persons — the prophetic and covenantal words of blessing spoken over individuals, communities, and Christ at his coming
groups.notes: Description updated to match verse_context_group correction — VCB-022 80-char truncation restored

groups.context_description: Term names blessing as the inner act of responding to curse with blessing — the counterintuitive orientation of the inner person that blesses persecutors
groups.notes: Description updated to match verse_context_group correction — VCB-022 80-char truncation restored

groups.context_description: Term names God's act of blessing — the sovereign covenantal gift of favour, fruitfulness, and life that flows from God's inner disposition of love toward his creation and people
groups.notes: Description updated to match verse_context_group correction — VCB-022 80-char truncation restored

groups.context_description: Term names the human act of blessing God — the inner-being orientation of worship, doxology, and praise that declares God worthy and exalts his name
groups.notes: Description updated to match verse_context_group correction — VCB-022 80-char truncation restored

groups.context_description: Term names the human act of blessing other humans — the covenantal declaration of favour, the prophetic word of blessing, and the communal invocation of God's goodness over persons and communities
groups.notes: Description updated to match verse_context_group correction — VCB-022 80-char truncation restored

groups.context_description: Term names the ironic/euphemistic use of blessing — cursing God under the name of "blessing" (the Job passages), or blessing idols — and the self-blessing of the sinful heart
groups.notes: Description updated to match verse_context_group correction — VCB-022 80-char truncation restored

groups.context_description: Term names blessedness as the inner state of the person who receives God's freely given righteousness — and the loss of that blessedness when the inner person turns back to works
groups.notes: Description updated to match verse_context_group correction — VCB-022 80-char truncation restored

groups.context_description: Term names the charisma as God's free gift of grace — the spiritual capacity given to each person for the building up of the community, and supremely the gift of eternal life
groups.notes: Description updated to match verse_context_group correction — VCB-022 80-char truncation restored
